title: Wolfspeed Announces Key Milestones and Operational Update
text: Wolfspeed reported significant progress on its silicon carbide manufacturing initiatives while disclosing a production setback at one facility. The company's Mohawk Valley fab reached 20% wafer start utilization and achieved LEED Silver certification for sustainability. This 200mm silicon carbide facility represents the first purpose-built, fully automated fab of its kind globally. Additionally, the supporting materials production facility hit its target to enable approximately 25% utilization at Mohawk Valley by year-end 2024. The John Palmour Manufacturing Center in Siler City, North Carolina successfully activated initial furnaces ahead of schedule. The facility is expected to achieve crystal qualification by early August 2024, positioning it to deliver materials to Mohawk Valley by summer 2025. However, the company experienced an equipment incident at its Durham 150mm device fab causing temporary capacity reduction. Production resumed, with the company anticipating normal capacity levels by August. Due to this disruption, Wolfspeed updated guidance: fourth quarter fiscal 2024 revenue remains unchanged at $185-$215 million, but gross margins were reduced to 0-8% non-GAAP range. The incident could impact first quarter 2025 revenue by approximately $20 million, with similar margin pressures expected. CEO Gregg Lowe stated that the company is well-positioned to continue executing its 200mm vertical integration strategy while acknowledging swift remediation of the Durham incident.
